title: chimera_report
text: chimera_report generated rchime() function data.frame 18 columns row sequence dataset. ’s brief description various columns.
code: 1. Score: a higher score means a more likely chimeric alignment. 2. Query: query sequence name. 3. ParentA: parent A sequence name. 4. ParentB: parent B sequence name. 5. Top_Parent: top parent sequence name (i.e. parent most similar to the query). 6. QM: percentage of similarity of query (Q) and model (M) constructed as a part of parent A and a part of parent B. 7. QA: percentage of similarity of query (Q) and parent A. 8. QB: percentage of similarity of query (Q) and parent B. 9. QAB: percentage of similarity of parent A and parent B. 10. QT percentage of similarity of query (Q) and top parent (T) 11. LY : yes votes in the left part of the model. 12. LN: no votes in the left part of the model. 13. LA: abstain votes in the left part of the model. 14. RY: yes votes in the right part of the model. 15. RN: no votes in the right part of the model. 16. RA: abstain votes in the right part of the model. 17. Div: divergence, defined as (QM - QT).  18. Chimeric_Status: query is chimeric (Y), or not (N), or is a borderline case (?).

title: chimera_report
text: Let’s run rchime() denovo approach generate chimera report, take closer look.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: rchime() function allows detect remove chimeric sequences using dataset ’s reference (denovo). denovo approach preferred method removing chimeras. rchime() can used strollur objects data.frames inputs. Let’s look examples data types using sequence data mothur’s MiSeq_SOP example analysis.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
code: fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance.rds"))  strollur <- strollur::new_dataset("rchime denovo example")  strollur::add(strollur, table = fasta_data, type = "sequence") #> Added 6084 sequences. strollur::assign(strollur, table = abundance_data, type = "sequence_abundance") #> Assigned 6084 sequence abundances.  strollur #> rchime denovo example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   3217.38 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  32164.75 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  64328.50 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  96492.25 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 125439.62 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 128655.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 6084  #> Total number of seqs: 128655  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
code: df <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_data_frame_by_sample.rds"))  str(df) #> 'data.frame':    11039 obs. of  4 variables: #>  $ sequence_name: chr  "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" ... #>  $ sequence     : chr  "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ ... #>  $ sample       : chr  "F3D2" "F3D146" "F3D149" "F3D150" ... #>  $ abundance    : int  222 1 1 1 127 17 32 13 95 86 ...

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: removing chimeras using denovo method, potential parents chosen abundant sequences dataset. remove chimeras let’s discuss dereplicate parameter. dataset includes sample assignments, recommend setting dereplicate parameter TRUE. dereplicate = FALSE, sequence flagged chimeric one sample, removed samples. experience suggests bit aggressive since ’ve seen rare sequences get flagged chimeric ’re abundant sequence another sample. conservative approach, set dereplicate = TRUE remove sequences samples flagged chimeric. Let’s use denovo method remove chimeras.
code: strollur_results <- rchime(strollur, dereplicate = TRUE) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> Added a chimera_report. #> → rchime removed `10453` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `9.1344587802887` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras.  strollur #> rchime denovo example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   2956.05 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  29551.50 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  59102.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  88652.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 115247.95 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 118202.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras   3588 10453 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 2496  #> Total number of seqs: 118202  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  data_frame_results <- rchime(df, dereplicate = TRUE) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> → rchime detected `10453` chimeras in your dataset. #> → It took `9.12996387481689` seconds to detect the chimeras.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: rchime() function returns list containing results function. running command strollur object, chimera_report added, chimeras removed automatically. Let’s take closer look results returned.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: chimera_report data.frame row sequence dataset. Let’s take look first 5 chimeric sequences report:

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: Results also contains list names chimeric sequences. Let’s get names first 10 chimeras.

title: Denovo Chimera Detection
text: Results contain set_abundance_values list dereplicate = TRUE removing chimeras automatically. set_abundance_values three items: ‘sequence_names’, ‘abundances’ ‘samples’. sequence dataset entry sequence_names abundances. abundance values parsed sample, order given set_abundance_values$samples. Let’s look first two sequences abundances detecting chimeras: can see abundances sequence M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460 remain meaning chimeric sample present . can see sequence M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10134_24617 found chimeric every sample included . Now let’s look example sequence found chimeric samples present . can see samples F3D0 F3D142 find sequence chimeric, F3D1, F3D2, F3D148 find chimeric abundance samples set 0.

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
text: rchime() function allows detect remove chimeras dataset using referenced based approach. rchime() can used strollur objects data.frames inputs. Let’s look examples data types using sequence data mothur’s MiSeq_SOP example analysis, SILVA reference sequences trimmed region interest reference. rchime designed flexible can use reference choose.

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
text: Let’s create strollur object contains SILVA reference sequences. Let’s create strollur object containing MiSeq_SOP sequences.
code: reference <- strollur::new_dataset("Silva V4 Region")  strollur::add(reference,   table = readRDS(rchime_example("reference.rds")),   type = "sequence" ) #> Added 14956 sequences.  reference #> Silva V4 Region: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns  numseqs #> Minimum:         1  270    270      0        3     0     1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  292    292      0        4     0   374.90 #> 25%-tile:        1  293    293      0        4     0  3740.00 #> Median:          1  293    293      0        4     0  7479.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  293    293      0        5     0 11218.00 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  294    294      1        6     1 14583.10 #> Maximum:         1  351    351      5        9     5 14956.00 #> Mean:            1  292    292      0        4     0     0.00 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 14956  #> Total number of seqs: 14956 fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance.rds"))  strollur <- strollur::new_dataset("rchime reference example")  strollur::add(strollur, table = fasta_data, type = "sequence") #> Added 6084 sequences. strollur::assign(strollur, table = abundance_data, type = "sequence_abundance") #> Assigned 6084 sequence abundances.  strollur #> rchime reference example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   3217.38 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  32164.75 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  64328.50 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  96492.25 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 125439.62 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 128655.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 6084  #> Total number of seqs: 128655  #> title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
code: reference_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("reference.rds"))  str(reference_data) #> 'data.frame':    14956 obs. of  2 variables: #>  $ sequence_name: chr  "DQ415841.1" "L02888.1" "AB037561.1" "AF355050.1" ... #>  $ sequence     : chr  "GTGCCAGCAGCCGCGGTAATACGGGGGGTGCAAGCGTTGTTCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGAGCGTGTAGGCTGTCTGATGTGTCAGATGTGAAAGCCCTGGGCTTAACCC"| __truncated__ "GTGCCAGCAGCCGCGGTAATACGAGAGGGCTAGCGTTATTCGGAATTATTGGGCGTAAAGGGCGCGTAGGCTGGTTAATAAGTTAAAAGTGAAATCCCGAGGCTTAACCTT"| __truncated__ "GTGCCAGCAGCCGCGGTAATACGAAGGGTGCAAGCGTTAATCGGAATTACTGGGCGTAAAGCGCGCGTAGGTGGTTCAGCAAGTTGGATGTGAAATCCCCGGGCTCAACCT"| __truncated__ "GTGCCAGCAGCCGCGGTAATACGGAGGGTGCAAGCGTTATTCGGAATCACTGGGCGTAAAGGACGCGTAGGCGGGTTGATAAGTCAGATGTGAAATCCTACAGCTTAACTG"| __truncated__ ...  data_df <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_data_frame.rds"))  str(data_df) #> 'data.frame':    6084 obs. of  3 variables: #>  $ sequence_name: chr  "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10133_8460" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10134_24617" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10331_23332" "M00967_43_000000000-A3JHG_1_1101_10340_12294" ... #>  $ sequence     : chr  "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGTAGGGGGCAAGCGTTATCCGGATTTACTGGGTGTAAAGGGAGCGTAGGCGGCCATGCAAGTCAGAAGTGAAAACCCGGGGCTCAACCCTGGGAGTGCTTTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGGAGGATGCGAGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGAGCGCAGGCGGCATGGCAAGTCAGATGTGAAAGCCCGGGGCTCAACCCCGGGACTGCATTTGAAACT"| __truncated__ "TACGGAGGATGCGAGCGTTATCCGGATTTATTGGGTTTAAAGGGTGCGTAGGCGGGCTGTTAAGTCAGCGGTCAAATGTCGGGGCTCAACGCCGTCGAGCCGTTGAAACTG"| __truncated__ ... #>  $ abundance    : int  620 4 1 1 1 1 1 3 1 2 ...

title: Reference Based Chimera Detection
text: removing chimeras using reference, potential parents chosen set reference sequences. Let’s use reference remove chimeras.
code: strollur_results <- rchime(strollur, reference = reference) #> Added a chimera_report. #> → rchime removed `4849` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `8.47708582878113` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras.  strollur #> rchime reference example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   3096.15 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  30952.50 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  61904.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  92855.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 120711.85 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 123806.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence chimeras_rchime   1676  4849 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 4408  #> Total number of seqs: 123806  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  data_frame_results <- rchime(data_df, reference = reference_data) #> → rchime detected `4849` chimeras in your dataset. #> → It took `8.50946164131165` seconds to detect the chimeras.

text: can install development version rchime GitHub :
code: # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("mothur/rchime")

title: R package for chimera detection
text: rchime() function accepts strollur objects data.frames inputs. Let’s create strollur::strollur object using files mothur’s Miseq_SOP example analysis. use denovo method rchime() detect remove chimeras dataset.
code: fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance.rds"))  data <- strollur::new_dataset("rchime denovo example")  strollur::add(data, table = fasta_data, type = "sequences") #> ℹ Added 6084 sequences. #> [1] 6084 strollur::assign(data, table = abundance_data, type = "sequence_abundance") #> ℹ Assigned 6084 sequence abundances. #> [1] 6084  chimera_report <- rchime(data) #>  #> The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> ℹ Added a chimera_report. #> → rchime removed `10453` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `4.26860308647156` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras.  data #> rchime denovo example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   2956.05 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  29551.50 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  59102.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  88652.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 115247.95 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 118202.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras   3588 10453 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 2496  #> Total number of seqs: 118202  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1

what: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: rchime() function allows detect chimeras data using denovo approach alternatively reference model. preferred way use abundant sequences reference (denovo). addition, sequence flagged chimeric one sample, (dereplicate=FALSE) sequence removed samples. experience suggests bit aggressive since ’ve seen rare sequences get flagged chimeric ’re abundant sequence another sample. conservative approach, set (dereplicate=TRUE) remove sequences samples flagged chimeric. function uses code vsearch tools.

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
code: # S3 method for class 'data.frame' rchime(   data,   reference = NULL,   dereplicate = TRUE,   silent = FALSE,   remove_chimeras = NULL,   rchime_options = NULL,   table_names = list(sequence_name = "sequence_name", sequence = "sequence", abundance =     "abundance", sample = "sample") )

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: data data.frame containing sequence data. reference data.frame containing reference sequence data. dereplicate, Boolean, dereplicate option allows flag chimeras sample. example, dereplicate parameter FALSE, one group finds sequence chimeric, flagged groups. dereplicate set TRUE, sequences found chimeric flagged sample found chimeric . Default = TRUE. silent Boolean, suppress console outputs. Default = FALSE. remove_chimeras used data strollur object. rchime_options List, can fine tune vsearch specific options using  [rchime_options()] function. Default = NULL. table_names, named list used indicate names columns data.frame. used data data.frame. default: table_names <- list(sequence_name = "sequence_name", sequence = "sequence" abundance = "abundance", sample = "sample") table_names, 'sequence_name' string containing name column 'table' contains sequence names. Default column name 'sequence_name'. table_names, 'sequence' string containing name column 'table' contains sequences. Default column name 'sequence'. table_names, 'abundance' string containing name column 'table' contains abundances. Default column name 'abundance'. table_names, 'sample' string containing name column 'table' contains samples. Default column name 'sample'.

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: list() containing chimera report, vector chimeric sequence's names. running multiple samples dereplicate = TRUE, table containing modified sequence abundances also returned.

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: Rognes T, Flouri T, Nichols B, Quince C, Mahé F. (2016) VSEARCH: versatile open source tool metagenomics. PeerJ 4:e2584. doi: 10.7717/peerj.2584 Edgar,R.C., Haas,B.J., Clemente,J.C., Quince,C. Knight,R. (2011), UCHIME improves sensitivity speed chimera detection. Bioinformatics 27:2194.

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
text: Sarah Westcott, swestcot@umich.edu

title: Detect chimeras in your data.frames. — rchime.data.frame
code: # Detect chimeras from the dataset using denovo approach by sample # (recommended)  data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_data_frame_by_sample.rds"))  chimera_report <- rchime(data, dereplicate = TRUE) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> → rchime detected `10453` chimeras in your dataset. #> → It took `9.13978219032288` seconds to detect the chimeras.  # Alternatively you can detect chimeras using a reference  reference <- readRDS(rchime_example("reference.rds")) data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_data_frame.rds"))  chimera_report <- rchime(data, reference = reference) #> → rchime detected `4849` chimeras in your dataset. #> → It took `8.52422738075256` seconds to detect the chimeras.

title: Detect and remove chimeras from your strollur dataset object. — rchime.strollur
code: # Let's load a strollur object named "rchime denovo example"  data_denovo <- strollur::load_dataset(   rchime_example("strollur_multi_sample.rds") )  # Detect and remove chimeras from the dataset using denovo approach by sample # (recommended)  chimera_report <- rchime(data_denovo, dereplicate = TRUE) #> ℹ The denovo method runs with a single processor. #> Added a chimera_report. #> → rchime removed `10453` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `9.09301161766052` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras. data_denovo #> rchime - strollur example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   2956.05 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  29551.50 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  59102.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  88652.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 115247.95 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 118202.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence rchime-chimeras   3588 10453 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 2496  #> Total number of seqs: 118202  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  #>   # Alternatively you can detect and remove chimeras using a reference  data_reference <- strollur::load_dataset(   rchime_example("strollur_multi_sample.rds") )  reference <- strollur::load_dataset(rchime_example("strollur_reference.rds"))  chimera_report <- rchime(data_reference, reference = reference) #> Added a chimera_report. #> → rchime removed `5325` chimeras from your dataset. #> → It took `8.38780736923218` seconds to detect and remove the chimeras. data_reference #> rchime - strollur example: #>  #>             starts ends nbases ambigs polymers numns   numseqs #> Minimum:         1  249    249      0        3     0      1.00 #> 2.5%-tile:       1  252    252      0        3     0   3084.25 #> 25%-tile:        1  252    252      0        4     0  30833.50 #> Median:          1  252    252      0        4     0  61666.00 #> 75%-tile:        1  253    253      0        5     0  92498.50 #> 97.5%-tile:      1  253    253      0        6     0 120247.75 #> Maximum:         1  256    256      0        8     0 123330.00 #> Mean:            1  252    252      0        4     0      0.00 #> scrap_summary: #>       type      trash_code unique total #> 1 sequence chimeras_rchime   1662  5325 #>  #> Number of unique seqs: 4422  #> Total number of seqs: 123330  #>  #> Total number of samples: 20  #> Total number of custom reports: 1  #>

title: Detects chimeras using a denovo method processing by sample. — rchimeDenovo
code: rchimeDenovo(sequence_name, sequence, abundance, options = NULL)

title: Detects chimeras using a denovo method processing by sample. — rchimeDenovo

text: list()

title: Detects chimeras using a denovo method processing by sample. — rchimeDenovo
code: # Read in data.frames containing sequence and abundance data  sequence_name <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_names_by_sample.rds")) sequence <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_sequences_by_sample.rds")) abundance <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_abundance_by_sample.rds"))  # Detect chimeras  options <- rchime_options(dereplicate = TRUE) results <- rchimeDenovo(sequence_name = sequence_name,                         sequence = sequence,                         abundance = abundance,                         options)

title: Detects chimeras using a denovo approach — rchimeDenovoSingleSample
code: # Read in data.frames containing sequence and abundance data  fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) abundance_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("single_sample_abundance.rds"))  # Detect chimeras  results <- rchimeDenovoSingleSample(sequence_name = fasta_data$sequence_name,                                     sequence = fasta_data$sequence,                                     abundance = abundance_data$abundance)

title: Detects chimeras using a reference based approach — rchimeReference

Default = NULL.

title: Detects chimeras using a reference based approach — rchimeReference
code: # Read in data.frames containing sequence data  fasta_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("miseq_fasta.rds")) reference_data <- readRDS(rchime_example("reference.rds"))  # Detect chimeras  results <- rchimeReference(sequence_name = fasta_data$sequence_name,                            sequence = fasta_data$sequence,                            reference_name = reference_data$sequence_name,                            reference_sequence = reference_data$sequence)

code: rchime_example() #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/rchime/extdata" rchime_example("reference.rds") #> [1] "/home/runner/work/_temp/Library/rchime/extdata/reference.rds"

what: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
text: rchime_options() function allows set parameters various chimera detection functions. exception dereplicate, recommend using defaults, unless compelling reason otherwise.

title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
code: rchime_options(   processors = parallelly::availableCores(),   dereplicate = TRUE,   abskew = 2,   minh = 0.28,   mindiv = 0.8,   xn = 8,   dn = 1.4,   maxp = 3 )

title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
text: processors Integer, number cores use. Default = available dereplicate, Boolean, dereplicate option allows remove chimeras sample. example, dereplicate parameter FALSE, one group finds sequence chimeric, removed groups. dereplicate set TRUE, sequences found chimeric removed sample found chimeric . Default = TRUE. abskew Float, minimum abundance skew (denovo ). Default = 2.0. abskew <- min (abund(parent1), abund(parent2)) / abund(query) minh Float, Mininum score report chimera. Default 0.28. Values 0.1 5 might reasonable. Lower values increase sensitivity may report false positives. decrease –xn, may need increase –minh, vice versa. mindiv Float, Minimum divergence ratio, default 0.8. Div ratio 100%% %%identity query sequence closest candidate parent. care close chimeras, increase –mindiv , say, 1.0 2.0, also decrease –min h, say 0.1, increase sensitivity. well works depend data. Best tune parameters good benchmark. xn Float, Weight vote, also called beta parameter. Default 8.0. Decreasing weight around 3 4 may give better performance denoised data. dn Float, Pseudo-count prior number votes. Default 1.4. Probably good reason change unless can retune good benchmark data. Reasonable values probably range 0.2 2. maxp Integer, Maximum number candidate parents consider. Default 3.

title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
text: List containing selected rchime parameters values.

title: The rchime_options() function allows you to set vsearch specific optional parameters. — rchime_options
